root_text.ud5.8:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.ud5.8:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena āyasmā ānando tadahuposathe pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya rājagahaṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi.
root_text.ud5.8:2.1: Addasā kho devadatto āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ rājagahe piṇḍāya carantaṁ.
root_text.ud5.8:2.2: Disvāna yenāyasmā ānando tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ ānandaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud5.8:2.3: “ajjatagge dānāhaṁ, āvuso ānanda, aññatreva bhagavatā aññatra bhikkhusaṅghā uposathaṁ karissāmi saṅghakammāni cā”ti.
root_text.ud5.8:3.1: Atha kho āyasmā ānando rājagahe piṇḍāya caritvā pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkanto yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi.
root_text.ud5.8:3.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud5.8:4.1: “Idhāhaṁ, bhante, pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya rājagahaṁ piṇḍāya pāvisiṁ.
root_text.ud5.8:4.2: Addasā kho maṁ, bhante, devadatto rājagahe piṇḍāya carantaṁ.
root_text.ud5.8:4.3: Disvāna yenāhaṁ tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā maṁ etadavoca:
root_text.ud5.8:4.4: ‘ajjatagge dānāhaṁ, āvuso ānanda, aññatreva bhagavatā aññatra bhikkhusaṅghā uposathaṁ karissāmi saṅghakammāni cā’ti.
root_text.ud5.8:4.5: Ajja, bhante, devadatto saṅghaṁ bhindissati, uposathañca karissati saṅghakammāni cā”ti.
root_text.ud5.8:5.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud5.8:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.ud5.8:1.3: Now at that time Venerable Ānanda robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, entered Rājagaha for alms.
translation_text.ud5.8:2.1: Devadatta saw him wandering for alms,
translation_text.ud5.8:2.2: so he went up to him and said,
translation_text.ud5.8:2.3: “From this day forth, Reverend Ānanda, I shall perform the sabbath and legal proceedings of the Saṅgha apart from the Buddha and the Saṅgha of mendicants.”
translation_text.ud5.8:3.1: Then Ānanda wandered for alms in Rājagaha. After the meal, on his return from almsround, he went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side,
translation_text.ud5.8:3.2: and told him what had just happened, adding:
translation_text.ud5.8:4.5: “Today, sir, Devadatta will split the Saṅgha. He will perform the sabbath and legal proceedings of the Saṅgha.”
translation_text.ud5.8:5.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud5.8:6.1: “It’s easy for the good to do good,
translation_text.ud5.8:6.2: but for the bad, doing good is hard.
translation_text.ud5.8:6.3: For the bad, it’s easy to do bad,
translation_text.ud5.8:6.4: but for the noble ones, doing bad is hard.”

variant_text.ud5.8:6.2: Sādhu pāpena dukkaraṁ → sādhuṁ pāpena dukkaraṁ (mr)
